question.text: A merchant banker fails to enter into an agreement with a client as required under SEBI regulations. Under which section of the SEBI Act would the penalty be imposed?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15B of the SEBI Act deals with the penalty for failure to enter into an agreement with clients. Section 15A covers failure to furnish information, Section 15C covers failure to redress investor grievances, and Section 15HB covers general contraventions. **The Trap:** Section 15A sounds similar but covers a different default (failure to furnish information/returns). Section 15C relates to investor grievance redressal. Candidates often confuse the specific penalty sections. **Cross-Exam Context:** These penalty sections are tested in NISM Series IX and are relevant to all intermediary certifications under SEBI. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Memorize: 15A = furnish info, 15B = enter agreement, 15C = redress grievances. These three sections form the basic compliance obligations for all SEBI-registered intermediaries.
